description: Fix all 4 TLA+ verification gaps (G-1 through G-4) so the API implementation matches the formal specification, bringing coverage from 84% to 100%

userStories.title: Fix G-1: Use sorted composite key for BurnBuddy docs
userStories.description: As a user, I want only one Burn Buddy relationship per friend pair so that accepting cross-requests (A→B and B→A) doesn't create duplicate records.
acceptanceCriteria: In services/api/src/routes/burn-buddies.ts, the accept handler creates the BurnBuddy doc with ID ${uid1}_${uid2} (sorted composite key) instead of randomUUID()
The BurnBuddy.id field also uses the sorted composite key (matching the doc ID)
If a BurnBuddy doc already exists for the pair (second accept of a cross-request), return 409 Conflict instead of creating a duplicate
Update the gap-documenting tests in burn-buddies.test.ts (G-1 section) to assert the correct behavior — doc ID IS the sorted composite key, and duplicate accept returns 409
Add a test: accepting a cross-request when a BurnBuddy already exists returns 409
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The friendship pattern in friends.ts uses sorted composite key ${uid1}_${uid2} as the Firestore doc ID — replicate this pattern for burn buddies. The accept handler is around line 92-134 in burn-buddies.ts. The gap-documenting tests are in the 'TLA+ Gap G-1' describe block around line 985.

userStories.title: Create requireProfile middleware and apply to burn-buddies routes
userStories.description: As a system, I need a requireProfile middleware that checks Firestore profile existence, and apply it to burn-buddy request creation as the first route to enforce CDI-1.
acceptanceCriteria: Create services/api/src/middleware/requireProfile.ts following the pattern of middleware/auth.ts
The middleware fetches the user profile from Firestore (users/{uid}) and returns 403 with { error: 'Profile required' } if it doesn't exist
Attach the profile to req.profile for downstream use, typed via Express augmentation in types/express.d.ts
Apply requireProfile after requireAuth on POST /burn-buddies/requests in burn-buddies.ts
Update the gap-documenting test in burn-buddies.test.ts (G-4 section) to assert 403 when no profile exists
Add a test verifying that burn-buddy request creation without a profile returns 403
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The middleware pattern is in services/api/src/middleware/auth.ts. Use getDb() from lib/firestore.ts for Firestore access. The Express type augmentation is in services/api/src/types/express.d.ts. Tests use vi.mock + vi.hoisted to stub Firestore. The G-4 gap tests are in the 'TLA+ Gap G-4' describe block in burn-buddies.test.ts.

userStories.title: Apply requireProfile to friends, burn-squads, and workouts routes
userStories.description: As a system, I need requireProfile applied to all remaining social action creation endpoints so that CDI-1 (ProfileRequiredForSocialActions) is fully enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply requireProfile after requireAuth on POST /friends/requests in friends.ts
Apply requireProfile after requireAuth on POST /burn-squads in burn-squads.ts
Apply requireProfile after requireAuth on POST /workouts in workouts.ts
Add a test in friends.test.ts verifying that friend request creation without a profile returns 403
Add a test in burn-squads.test.ts verifying that squad creation without a profile returns 403
Add a test in workouts.test.ts verifying that workout creation without a profile returns 403
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern established in US-002 for burn-buddies. Each test file uses vi.mock + vi.hoisted — add the profile doc mock to beforeEach and add a test case that omits the profile. Check existing gap-documenting G-4 tests in friends.test.ts, burn-squads.test.ts, workouts.test.ts and update them to assert 403.

userStories.title: Fix G-3: Username uniqueness race condition
userStories.description: As a user, I want my username to be truly unique so that concurrent profile creations can't claim the same username.
acceptanceCriteria: In services/api/src/lib/username.ts, use Firestore doc.create() (which fails if doc already exists) instead of doc.set() for the username reservation, OR wrap the read-check-write in a Firestore transaction
In services/api/src/routes/users.ts, the profile creation flow handles the ALREADY_EXISTS error from doc.create() by retrying with the next suffix
Add a unit test that simulates a concurrent claim (mock doc.create() to fail with ALREADY_EXISTS on first attempt, succeed on retry)
Existing username-related tests still pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: generateUniqueUsername() is at username.ts:38-63. It does a read-then-write without a transaction. The profile creation batch is in users.ts around line 129-133. Firestore doc.create() throws error code 6 (ALREADY_EXISTS) if the document exists. The existing tests mock Firestore via vi.mock + vi.hoisted.

userStories.title: Fix G-2: Explicit self-invite check in squad member add
userStories.description: As a system, I need an explicit guard against self-invites in squad member additions so the invariant doesn't rely on a cross-domain assumption.
acceptanceCriteria: In services/api/src/routes/burn-squads.ts, add if (memberUid === uid) return 400 with { error: 'Cannot invite yourself' } before the friendship check in POST /:id/members
Add a test in burn-squads.test.ts: attempting to add yourself as a member returns 400 with the correct error message
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The POST /:id/members handler is at burn-squads.ts around line 330-386. Add the self-invite check right after the memberUid validation (around line 340), before the squad doc lookup and friendship check.

userStories.title: Update TLA+ verification report to 100% coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the verification report to reflect all gaps as fixed so it stays accurate and trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Update specs/tla/VERIFICATION_REPORT.md — change G-1 (AtMostOneBuddyPerPair) from ⚠️ to ✅ with note about sorted composite key fix
Change G-2 (NoSelfInvites) from ⚠️ to ✅ with note about explicit self-invite check
Change G-3 (UsernameUniqueness) from ⚠️ to ✅ with note about doc.create() fix
Change G-4 (ProfileRequiredForSocialActions) from ❌ to ✅ with note about requireProfile middleware
Update the Gap Severity Summary table to show all gaps as resolved
Mark the Summary of Gaps section as resolved (e.g., prefix each gap heading with 'RESOLVED')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The verification report is at specs/tla/VERIFICATION_REPORT.md. Update the status column in each domain table (sections 2, 3, 5, 7) and the detailed gap descriptions at the bottom.
